title: Savory Steamed Norwegian Fish Stew
description: A low-fat Norwegian dish featuring fish, pickle juice, and steamed to perfection in a banana leaf, this stew is a briny and juicy meal prep option.
instructions: Rinse the cod fish pieces under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a large bowl, whisk together pickle juice, water, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cod fish pieces to the bowl and marinate for 10 minutes.
Prepare the banana leaves by cutting them into 12-inch squares and washing them under cold water.
In the center of each banana leaf square, place a piece of cod fish, some sliced carrots, cubed potatoes, and chopped onion.
Pour some of the marinade over the fish and vegetables, making sure everything is well coated.
Fold the banana leaves over the filling to form a tight package and secure with a toothpick if needed.
Place the packages in a large steamer basket and steam over boiling water for 15 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
